description_long: This object is a partially peeled orange, with its thick, textured rind splayed outward in several large, uneven segments. The inner fruit is exposed, revealing plump, juicy segments that are a lighter orange color and have a slightly glossy, moist appearance. The rind is rough and dimpled, with a matte finish and a natural, organic shape that curves outward from the base. The interior segments are tightly packed, with visible fibrous membranes separating each section. The overall form is irregular, with the rind curling away from the fruit in a way that suggests it was peeled by hand. The coloration transitions from a deeper orange on the rind to a paler, almost translucent orange on the fruit segments.
description: A partially peeled orange with its rough rind splayed outward, exposing the plump, moist fruit segments inside. The rind is deep orange and matte, while the inner fruit is lighter and glossy.
description_view_0: The orange is seen with three large rind sections curling outward, revealing the central fruit and several visible segments.
description_view_1: This view highlights the open side of the orange, showing more of the exposed fruit segments and the inner surface of the rind.
description_view_2: The orange appears with the rind more widely spread, offering a clear look at the fibrous membranes between the fruit segments.
description_view_3: The rind is more prominent in this view, with the fruit segments partially obscured and the outer texture of the peel clearly visible.
attribute_location_or_placement: sidewalk, street, park bench, or outdoor urban ground
